completed_steps.summary: Reclassified Lens.Core.Src as a single monolith toolkit source project with one primary part and a published adapter layer plus module core
Inventoried existing parent, module, script, and adapter documentation and used it to scope the exhaustive rescan
Confirmed the source stack as YAML, Markdown, Python, Node.js, GitHub Actions, and Git with manifest, registry, and packaging boundaries
Scanned adapter, prompt, skill, script, test, contract, and documentation surfaces and confirmed API, database, and UI artifacts are intentionally not applicable
Generated an expanded source tree analysis covering the adapter surface, module core, scripts, tests, contracts, templates, and parent docs
Captured contributor workflow, validated commands, release packaging flow, and the source-root preflight caveat
Regenerated the parent-level architecture document with clearer authored-source, adapter, registry, and release-pipeline boundaries
Wrote the updated overview, component inventory, development guide, deployment guide, and a new contribution guide
Regenerated the master index with exhaustive deep-link coverage into the embedded module docs
Validated the scan report JSON, checked all relative markdown links, and verified documented validation commands from the source root where applicable

project_parts.role: Authoritative source for the lens-work module and checked-in release adapter assets

project_classification.summary: Lens.Core.Src is a monolith source project that publishes a BMAD lifecycle toolkit and adapter surface rather than an HTTP service or UI application.
project_classification.architecture_pattern: source-authoritative module with adapter overlay and release-pipeline promotion

existing_documentation.summary: The project already carried a deep embedded doc set under _bmad/lens-work/docs plus source-side adapter instructions under .github; this rescan refreshed the parent-level source-project guide layer.

technology_stack.summary: YAML contracts, Markdown prompts/skills/docs, Python operational scripts, a Node.js module installer, GitHub Actions promotion, and Git-based state.

batches_completed.summary: Reviewed the published GitHub Copilot agent wrapper surface.
Reviewed the checked-in published prompt stubs that overlay release output.
Reviewed the thin wrapper skills used by the adapter layer.
Reviewed the promote-to-release workflow as part of the source architecture.
Reviewed the Lens bridge config used by wrapper flows.
Reviewed the authored runtime agent definitions.
Reviewed the module skill surface and its role as the primary behavior catalog.
Reviewed the authored module prompt surface behind the published stubs.
Reviewed installation, preflight, bootstrap, PR, state, and validation scripts.
Reviewed pytest-based script and contract validation modules.
Reviewed markdown contract references for topology, governance, provider adapters, sensing, and handoffs.
Reviewed authored planning and documentation templates.
Reviewed the embedded module documentation library to keep the parent docs complementary rather than duplicative.

development.summary: Contributors work in Lens.Core.Src, validate targeted changes locally, then rely on promotion plus downstream preflight for end-to-end verification.

deployment.summary: Promotion packages the source tree into Lens.Core.Release, commits to alpha, and manages the PR path toward beta before downstream control repos pull lens.core.

constraints.summary: Do not edit lens.core directly, keep published .github assets aligned with module source and installer output, and do not treat source-root preflight as the final validation path.

resume_instructions: Full comprehensive scan complete. Re-run as a full rescan when the source tree, published adapter surface, release workflow, or module registry changes materially.
